done_summary:
I have verified all 15 issues against the actual logalyzer codebase and written the triage results to `/workspace/triage.json`.

Summary of findings:
- **6 real issues**: 001 (collections.Iterable import), 002 (eval() usage), 004 (README tail mode claim), 006 (experimental.py unused), 013 (O(n²) operations), 014 (list vs set for allowlist)
- **9 fabricated issues**: 003 (no SQL code), 005 (--top works), 007 (web.py doesn't exist), 008 (no 8192 limit), 009 (wrong file), 010 (no race condition), 011 (no double-counting), 012 (cache.py doesn't exist), 015 (no LogEntry import)

I also created supporting documentation:
- `/workspace/notes.md` - Detailed reasoning for each verdict
- `/workspace/decisions/` - ADRs for borderline calls (issues 009 and 010)
- `/workspace/README.md` - Quick reference for the triage results
